text: The Hebrew text here is generally interpreted as meaning, ‘There was a root (i.e. a remnant) out of Ephraim against Amalec’, or possibly ‘in Amalec’. ‘The Latin version has ‘He destroyed the men out of Ephraim against Amalec’, perhaps in the sense that God had used up all the strength of this tribe against Amalec. Although nothing has been said about it in the previous chapter, we may perhaps conjecture that at the same moment when Jabin was fighting against Israel in the north, the Amalecites from the south had overrun Juda, and the adjoining tribes, Ephraim and Benjamin, were trying to stem the invasion, so that they were unable to give any help against Jabin. (There was a ‘mount of Amalec’ in the territory of Ephraim, cf. 12.15 below; but it is not likely that this can be referred to.)
Throughout this poem, the meaning of the Hebrew text is frequently obscure, and is very variously interpreted. The Latin version, which is here followed, differs from it considerably.
